channel.title: Getting Started on MCP Toolbox for Databases
channel.description: Recent content in Getting Started on MCP Toolbox for Databases

item.title: Python Quickstart (Local)
item.description:
Before you begin

This guide assumes you have already done the following:

  1. Installed Python (3.10 or higher) (including pip and your preferred virtual environment tool for managing dependencies e.g. venv).
  2. Installed PostgreSQL 16+ and the psql client.

Cloud Setup (Optional)

If you plan to use Google Cloud’s Vertex AI with your agent (e.g., using vertexai=True or a Google GenAI model), follow these one-time setup steps for local development:

  1. Install the Google Cloud CLI

item.title: JS Quickstart (Local)
item.description:
Before you begin

This guide assumes you have already done the following:

  1. Installed Node.js (v18 or higher).
  2. Installed PostgreSQL 16+ and the psql client.

Cloud Setup (Optional)

If you plan to use Google Cloud’s Vertex AI with your agent (e.g., using vertexai=True or a Google GenAI model), follow these one-time setup steps for local development:

  1. Install the Google Cloud CLI

  2. Set up Application Default Credentials (ADC)

  3. Set your project and enable Vertex AI

item.title: Go Quickstart (Local)
item.description:
Before you begin

This guide assumes you have already done the following:

  1. Installed Go (v1.24.2 or higher).
  2. Installed PostgreSQL 16+ and the psql client.

Cloud Setup (Optional)

If you plan to use Google Cloud’s Vertex AI with your agent (e.g., using vertexai=True or a Google GenAI model), follow these one-time setup steps for local development:

  1. Install the Google Cloud CLI

  2. Set up Application Default Credentials (ADC)

  3. Set your project and enable Vertex AI
